skill.name: omc
skill.description: oh-my-claudecode — Teams-first multi-agent orchestration layer for Claude Code. 32 specialized agents, smart model routing, persistent execution loops, and real-time HUD visibility. Zero learning curve.
skill.summary: Multi-agent orchestration for Claude Code with 32 specialized agents and intelligent task distribution.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 67 patterns that are all false positives. External command detections are Markdown code blocks in documentation, not executable code. Network findings are documentation URLs. Filesystem references are configuration instructions for users. No executable code or security risks present.

content.user_title: Orchestrate multi-agent workflows in Claude Code
content.value_statement: Complex tasks require coordinated effort across multiple AI agents. OMC provides teams-first orchestration with 32 specialized agents and smart model routing for efficient task completion.
actual_capabilities: Deploy 32 specialized agents with intelligent task distribution
Route tasks across model tiers from Haiku to Opus for cost optimization
Execute persistent workflows with verify/fix loops until completion
Enable parallel agent execution for maximum throughput
Provide real-time HUD visibility into agent activities
Support natural language interfaces for all orchestration modes
limitations: Requires Claude Code as the primary execution environment
Team orchestration features optimized for collaborative workflows
External AI integrations like Gemini CLI and Codex CLI are optional additions
Full feature set requires initial setup configuration

use_cases.title: Full-stack feature development
use_cases.description: Deploy team mode to coordinate planning, design, implementation, and verification across specialized agents for complete feature delivery.

use_cases.title: Autonomous task completion
use_cases.description: Use autopilot mode for end-to-end execution of well-defined tasks with minimal human oversight required.
use_cases.target_user: Individual developers seeking AI pair programming

use_cases.title: Large-scale refactoring
use_cases.description: Leverage ultrawork mode for maximum parallelism when fixing errors or refactoring across multiple files simultaneously.

prompt_templates.title: Basic autopilot task
prompt_templates.prompt: autopilot: build a REST API for managing tasks with CRUD operations
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new feature with clear requirements

prompt_templates.title: Team-based error resolution
prompt_templates.prompt: /omc:team 3:executor "fix all TypeScript errors in the project"
prompt_templates.scenario: Multiple errors requiring coordinated debugging effort

prompt_templates.title: Persistent refactoring loop
prompt_templates.prompt: ralph: refactor the authentication module to use JWT tokens
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex refactoring requiring verification and iterative fixes

prompt_templates.title: Parallel bug fixes
prompt_templates.prompt: ulw fix all linting errors and code style violations
prompt_templates.scenario: Many independent fixes that can execute in parallel

output_examples.input: Team mode: fix all TypeScript errors
output_examples.output: Staged pipeline executes: plan identifies error patterns, PRD defines fix strategy, exec agents resolve errors in parallel, verify confirms fixes, fix loop addresses any remaining issues until all errors resolved.
Single lead agent autonomously plans architecture, designs components, implements full-stack code, writes tests, and verifies functionality before presenting completed application.

best_practices: Use team mode for complex multi-step tasks requiring coordinated effort across specialized agents
Enable CLAUDE_CODE_EXPERIMENTAL_AGENT_TEAMS in settings for native team support
Run omc-doctor command to diagnose and resolve any configuration issues
anti_patterns: Using swarm or ultrapilot keywords unnecessarily as they are legacy facades that route to Team mode
Running parallel modes without understanding that ralph already includes ultrawork functionality
Expecting external AI integrations to work without installing optional Gemini CLI or Codex CLI packages

faq.question: How do I install OMC?
faq.answer: Run /plugin marketplace add https://github.com/Yeachan-Heo/oh-my-claudecode followed by /plugin install oh-my-claudecode, then run /omc:omc-setup to initialize.

faq.question: What is the difference between team mode and autopilot?
faq.answer: Team mode uses a staged pipeline with multiple specialized agents working collaboratively. Autopilot uses a single lead agent that handles all aspects of a task autonomously.

faq.question: Do I need to install external AI tools like Gemini CLI?
faq.answer: No, external AI integrations are optional for cross-validation. OMC works fully with Claude Code alone.

faq.question: How do I update OMC to the latest version?
faq.answer: Run /plugin marketplace update omc followed by /omc:omc-setup. If you encounter issues, run /omc:omc-doctor for diagnostics.

faq.question: What does the ralph mode do?
faq.answer: Ralph provides persistent execution with automatic verify/fix loops. It includes ultrawork functionality for parallel execution, ensuring tasks complete fully before finishing.

faq.question: How many agents can run in parallel?
faq.answer: OMC supports up to 32 specialized agents with smart model routing. The actual number used depends on task complexity and your configuration.
